_comment: Known, already-existing migration drift — accepted by npm run check:migrations so that
NEW drift fails CI instead of drowning in a permanently red check.
Every entry below is real: the objects were confirmed absent in production on 2026-07-20
via a direct PostgREST request (HTTP 404 / PGRST205), not just via the OpenAPI schema cache.
Each one is referenced by live application code, so each is a latent runtime failure of the
deploy.yml runs no migrations, so these have to be applied out-of-band (Supabase dashboard
SQL editor). Apply one, then DELETE its entry here — the check fails on stale entries, so
this file cannot quietly outlive the problem it documents.
Run `node scripts/check-migration-drift.mjs --strict` to fail on these too.

known_drift.003_subscribers.sql: Live subscribers table has a different shape (market, confirmed) and lacks status/source/country_code/user_agent/resubscribed_at/created_at/updated_at. 022_security_and_performance.sql already redefines the newsletter_subscribers view against the live shape, so the app works — but 003 as written was never applied.
known_drift.004_email_sequences.sql: email_sequence_logs absent (HTTP 404/PGRST205) plus 3 columns missing on subscribers. Used by app/api/webhooks/resend/route.ts and lib/email/nurture-sequence.ts.
known_drift.20260309100000_xray_score.sql: product_profiles and xray_results absent (HTTP 404/PGRST205). Used by lib/xray/* and app/api/xray/*.
known_drift.20260524190000_compliance_audit_runs.sql: compliance_audit_runs absent (HTTP 404/PGRST205). Used by lib/actions/compliance-audit.ts and the system-integrity dashboard widget.
